title: ulex × breoganii
constituents: Ulex × breoganii has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents, including isorhapontigenin, kaempferol, and quercetin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, helping to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The alkaloids present in Ulex × breoganii, such as harman and norharman, have also been found to have antispasmodic and sedative effects, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. By harnessing these medicinal constituents, Ulex × breoganii offers potential benefits for individuals seeking to manage stress, alleviate pain, and maintain overall well-being.
side_effects: Ulex × breoganii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of glycosides. In addition, improper use of the plant may lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory problems. Some individuals may also experience cardiovascular issues, including an increased heart rate and blood pressure, if the plant is ingested in large quantities. Furthermore, excessive consumption of Ulex × breoganii may lead to kidney damage and other organ problems over time.
